Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# WP-009 — CMD-V3-01: Command-bundle apply API
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply`
4	**Effort:** XL — ~4 weeks
5	**State:** `now`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T9 CMD-V3-01 (line 3136), §A Agent Instructions, §L Locked Decisions
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — `try_commit_bundle` (line 5001); the existing bundle-apply primitive this WP wraps
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — existing route stubs; WP-009 replaces the stub at `POST /api/models/{model_id}/commands/bundle`
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — WP-003 (API-V3-01) shipped; the `apply-bundle` `ToolDescriptor` stub must be updated with the real schema
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/cli/cli.mjs` — `apply-bundle` stub (lines 1075–1084); WP-009 replaces it
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/tkn/` — WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) shipped; `try_commit_bundle` already calls `_assert_tkn_round_trip`
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Verbatim CMD-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T9, line 3136)
24	
25	**CMD-V3-01 — Command-bundle apply API**
26	
27	_Source: User reframe 2026-05-08 ("the AI features are designed as API / CLI tools"); §L Locked Decisions (deterministic kernel boundary). Reframes the previous "agent edit loop" WP into a kernel API — the loop logic moves out to the agent; we ship the deterministic apply primitive._
28	
29	**Status.** `now`. **Scope.** A deterministic kernel API + CLI where any caller submits a `CommandBundle{ commands, assumptions, parent_revision, targetOptionId? }` and gets back `BundleResult{ applied, violations, new_revision }`. The "loop" — propose → dry-run → read advisories → repair → commit — is what an _agent_ does using this API. We ship the API, not the loop. This is the cleanest expression of §L: bim-ai's binary contains zero AI; the agent composes calls using its own reasoning.
30	
31	Three call modes: (1) `--dry-run` — runs SKB-05 + SKB-11 + SKB-19 + soundness pack against a hypothetical post-bundle state; returns advisories + checkpoint snapshot id; **does not mutate**. Idempotent. (2) `--commit` — same validation, then commits if no blocking advisories fire; returns new revision id; on failure, returns advisories and the bundle is _not_ applied. (3) `--commit --tolerate <advisory-class>` — explicit override; each tolerance recorded in audit log.
32	
33	Every bundle MUST include a non-empty `assumptions` field (CMD-V3-02 contract), `parent_revision`, and target a Design Option that is not `main` (OPT-V3-01 invariant). The kernel rejects violators with structured advisories.
34	
35	**Data model.**
36	```ts
37	type CommandBundle = {
38	  schemaVersion: 'cmd-v3.0';
39	  commands: KernelCommand[];
40	  assumptions: AssumptionEntry[];            // CMD-V3-02 — non-empty
41	  parentRevision: string;                    // optimistic concurrency
42	  targetOptionId?: string;                   // OPT-V3-01 — defaults to a fresh option
43	  tolerances?: Array<{ advisoryClass: string; reason: string }>;
44	};
45	type BundleResult = {
46	  schemaVersion: 'cmd-v3.0';
47	  applied: boolean;
48	  newRevision?: string;
49	  optionId?: string;
50	  violations: Advisory[];
51	  checkpointSnapshotId?: string;             // hand-off to VG-V3-01
52	};
53	```
54	
55	**Engine.** New module `app/bim_ai/cmd/` with `apply_bundle(bundle, mode) → BundleResult`. Wraps kernel command application with the validation + assumption + revision contract. CLI: `bim-ai apply-bundle <bundle.json> --base <revision> [--dry-run | --commit]`. REST: `POST /api/v3/bundles`. Both registered per API-V3-01. Idempotent: same bundle replayed against the same parent yields the same new revision. **UI.** No direct UI. **Acceptance.** (a) Dry-run returns advisories + checkpoint id; kernel state unchanged. (b) `--commit` produces new revision + new option id; `main` untouched. (c) Bundle missing `assumptions` rejected with `assumption_log_required`. (d) Stale `parent_revision` rejected with `revision_conflict`. (e) Idempotent replay returns existing revision id with `applied: false`. (f) An external agent using only `bim-ai apply-bundle` + `bim-ai compare` can complete the 12-step refinement fixture (TST-V3-01). **Effort.** XL — 4 weeks. **Cross-theme references.** API-V3-01. T1 KRN-V3-04 (every commit lands as an option per OPT-V3-01). T5 (catalogKey resolution at apply time). CQ-04 (`engine.py` split must complete first).
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Setup
60	
61	```bash
62	git checkout main && git pull
63	git checkout -b feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply
64	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
65	python -c "from bim_ai.tkn import encode, decode, diff; print('tkn ok')"
66	python -c "from bim_ai.api.registry import get_catalog; print(get_catalog().schemaVersion)"
67	uv sync && pnpm install
68	```
69	
70	---
71	
72	## Implementation tasks
73	
74	### 1. `CommandBundle` + `BundleResult` Pydantic types — `app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py`
75	
76	- `CommandBundle`: `schema_version: Literal["cmd-v3.0"]`, `commands: list[dict]`, `assumptions: list[AssumptionEntry]` (`min_length=1`), `parent_revision: int`, `target_option_id: str | None = None`, `tolerances: list[ToleranceEntry] | None = None`. All fields with camelCase `alias`.
77	- `BundleResult`: `schema_version`, `applied: bool`, `new_revision: int | None`, `option_id: str | None`, `violations: list[dict]`, `checkpoint_snapshot_id: str | None`.
78	- `AssumptionEntry` (CMD-V3-02 shape): `key: str`, `value: str | int | float | bool`, `confidence: float` (ge=0, le=1), `source: str`, `contestable: bool = True`, `evidence: str | None = None`.
79	- All models: `model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)`.
80	
81	### 2. Dry-run / commit / tolerate modes — `app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`
82	
83	Public signature: `def apply_bundle(doc: Document, bundle: CommandBundle, mode: Literal["dry_run", "commit"]) -> BundleResult`.
84	
85	Ordered steps:
86	1. `_validate_assumptions(bundle.assumptions)` — return `assumption_log_required` / `assumption_log_malformed` / `assumption_log_duplicate_key` on failure.
87	2. Revision guard: `bundle.parent_revision != doc.revision` → `revision_conflict`.
88	3. `targetOptionId` guard (task 4).
89	4. Call `engine.try_commit_bundle(doc, bundle.commands)`.
90	5. Compute `checkpoint_snapshot_id` = SHA-256 of `sort_keys=True` JSON of post-bundle elements wire (use `doc` elements on dry-run failure, `new_doc` elements on success).
91	6. Mode branch: `dry_run` → return without persisting; `commit` + `ok=True` → return `BundleResult(applied=True, ...)`; `commit` + `ok=False` → return `applied=False`.
92	
93	`apply_bundle` is **pure** — no DB I/O. Route layer handles persistence.
94	
95	### 3. `parent_revision` optimistic-lock guard
96	
97	Guard in step 2 above. Route layer (task 6) must additionally use a conditional DB update (`UPDATE model_records SET revision=N+1 WHERE id=X AND revision=N`); if 0 rows updated, return 409 `revision_conflict`.
98	
99	### 4. `targetOptionId` routing
100	
101	- Absent / `None` → proceed normally (commits to current model state; full OPT-V3-01 branching deferred to KRN-V3-04).
102	- `"main"` → reject with `direct_main_commit_forbidden`.
103	- Any other non-null value → reject with `option_routing_not_yet_implemented` + pointer to OPT-V3-01.
104	- Mark both branches `# TODO(OPT-V3-01)`.
105	
106	### 5. `assumptions` structural validation
107	
108	`_validate_assumptions` returns advisory dicts. Rules: non-empty array, each entry has `key` (non-empty), `confidence` in [0,1] (else `assumption_log_malformed` with entry index), unique keys (else `assumption_log_duplicate_key`). Kernel validates structure only — semantics live in the agent.
109	
110	### 6. REST route `POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles`
111	
112	Add to `app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`:
113	
114	```python
115	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/bundles")
116	async def apply_bundle_route(model_id: UUID, body: CommandBundleRequest,
117	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session), hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub)) -> dict[str, Any]:
118	```
119	
120	Flow: load row (404 if missing) → call `apply_bundle(doc, body.bundle, mode)` → on `applied=True` persist (`ModelRecord.document`, `.revision`), write `UndoStackRecord`, broadcast delta → return `result.model_dump(by_alias=True)`. HTTP 409 on `revision_conflict` or `assumption_log_required`. HTTP 200 for dry-run pass.
121	
122	Keep `POST /api/models/{model_id}/commands/bundle` alive as a compatibility shim — do not remove it.
123	
124	Update the `apply-bundle` `ToolDescriptor` in `app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`: add `assumptions`, `parentRevision`, `targetOptionId`, `tolerances` to `inputSchema`; update `outputSchema` to `BundleResult`; change `restEndpoint.path` to `/api/models/{model_id}/bundles`; update `cliExample` and `agentSafetyNotes`.
125	
126	### 7. CLI `apply-bundle` replacing the stub
127	
128	Replace the handler at line 1075 of `packages/cli/cli.mjs`. New arg shape:
129	
130	```
131	bim-ai apply-bundle [file|-] --base <revision> [--dry-run | --commit]
132	                              [--tolerate <advisory-class>]...
133	                              [--assumptions <file>]
134	```
135	
136	- `--base <revision>` required; maps to `parentRevision`.
137	- Default mode: `--dry-run` (agent safety — force explicit `--commit`).
138	- Legacy input (bare array or `{ commands: [] }`) auto-injects a synthetic `assumptions` entry with `confidence: 0`, `source: "cli-legacy"` and warns on stderr.
139	- POST to `/api/models/{model_id}/bundles`. Exit codes: 0 success, 2 `revision_conflict`, 3 `assumption_log_required`/`malformed`, 1 other error.
140	- Update `usage()` string.
141	
142	### 8. Tests
143	
144	- `app/tests/cmd/test_apply_bundle_types.py` — Pydantic round-trips; malformed `AssumptionEntry` rejection.
145	- `app/tests/cmd/test_apply_bundle_engine.py` — dry-run (state unchanged, checkpoint present), commit (revision+1, applied=true), missing assumptions, stale revision, `targetOptionId="main"`, tolerate mode, idempotent replay.
146	- `app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py` — FastAPI `TestClient`: 200 dry-run, 200 commit, 409 conflict, 409 missing assumptions, 422 malformed body.
147	- `packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh` — create model → dry-run → commit → verify revision incremented via `bim-ai snapshot`.
148	
149	---
150	
151	## Implementation rules (§A and §L)
152	
153	- **No AI in any binary.** `apply_bundle` and `try_commit_bundle` are fully deterministic. No LLM, no inference.
154	- **API-V3-01 contract.** Update the existing `apply-bundle` stub in `registry.py` — do not register a second descriptor.
155	- **Assumptions-first (§L).** Kernel rejects structurally invalid bundles. Human scripts and agents satisfy the same contract.
156	- **Deterministic outputs.** Same bundle + same parent revision → same `BundleResult` every time.
157	- **No silent main mutation.** `targetOptionId: "main"` always rejected (guard is permanent, not a TODO).
158	- **Backwards compatibility.** Legacy `POST /api/models/{model_id}/commands/bundle` endpoint must continue to work.
159	- **Do not split `engine.py`.** CQ-04 is a separate WP. Import `try_commit_bundle` from `bim_ai.engine` as-is; all new logic lives in `app/bim_ai/cmd/`.
160	
161	---
162	
163	## Verify gate
164	
165	```bash
166	pytest -x app/tests/cmd/ app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py -v
167	pytest -x app/tests/ -v                                   # no regressions
168	pnpm typecheck
169	pnpm format:check
170	# With server running on :8500:
171	BIM_AI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8500 bash packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh
172	curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8500/api/v3/tools/apply-bundle | python -m json.tool
173	```
174	
175	All six acceptance criteria must be demonstrable interactively: (a) dry-run unchanged state + checkpoint id, (b) commit → applied+revision, (c) missing assumptions → `assumption_log_required`, (d) stale revision → `revision_conflict`, (e) idempotent replay → `revision_conflict`, (f) smoke test passes.
176	
177	---
178	
179	## Commit + push
180	
181	```bash
182	git add app/bim_ai/cmd/ app/bim_ai/routes_api.py app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
183	        packages/cli/cli.mjs app/tests/cmd/ app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py \
184	        packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh
185	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
186	feat(cmd): CMD-V3-01 apply-bundle API + CLI (WP-009)
187	
188	New app/bim_ai/cmd/ module: CommandBundle + BundleResult Pydantic types,
189	AssumptionEntry structural validation, apply_bundle() pure function.
190	Three modes: dry_run (idempotent), commit, commit+tolerate.
191	parent_revision optimistic-lock, targetOptionId guards.
192	REST: POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles; legacy endpoint preserved.
193	CLI: --base, --dry-run|--commit, --tolerate, --assumptions; default dry-run.
194	ToolDescriptor in registry.py updated with full CMD-V3-01 schema.
195	
196	Replaces WP-003 apply-bundle stub. Depends on WP-003 + WP-005.
197	
198	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
199	EOF
200	)"
201	git push -u origin feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply
202	```
203	
204	---
205	
206	## Final-report template
207	
208	Paste into the PR description when merging:
209	
210	```
211	## WP-009 CMD-V3-01 — ship report
212	
213	Branch: feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply  |  PR: <URL>
214	
215	Acceptance criteria
216	- [x] (a) Dry-run: advisories + checkpoint id; state unchanged
217	- [x] (b) --commit: applied:true, revision+1
218	- [x] (c) Missing assumptions → assumption_log_required
219	- [x] (d) Stale parent_revision → revision_conflict
220	- [x] (e) Idempotent replay → revision_conflict
221	- [x] (f) CLI smoke test: apply-bundle round-trip passes
222	
223	New files: app/bim_ai/cmd/__init__.py, types.py, apply_bundle.py;
224	           app/tests/cmd/test_apply_bundle_types.py, test_apply_bundle_engine.py;
225	           app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py;
226	           packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh
227	Modified:  routes_api.py, api/registry.py, cli.mjs
228	
229	Deferred (TODO markers):
230	- OPT-V3-01: fresh DesignOption routing (requires KRN-V3-04)
231	- TST-V3-01: full 12-step refinement harness (follow-on WP)
232	- Audit log for tolerances (T3 activity stream WP)

# WP-009 — CMD-V3-01: Command-bundle apply API

**Branch:** `feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply`
**Effort:** XL — ~4 weeks
**State:** `now`

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T9 CMD-V3-01 (line 3136), §A Agent Instructions, §L Locked Decisions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — `try_commit_bundle` (line 5001); the existing bundle-apply primitive this WP wraps
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — existing route stubs; WP-009 replaces the stub at `POST /api/models/{model_id}/commands/bundle`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — WP-003 (API-V3-01) shipped; the `apply-bundle` `ToolDescriptor` stub must be updated with the real schema
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/cli/cli.mjs` — `apply-bundle` stub (lines 1075–1084); WP-009 replaces it
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/tkn/` — WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) shipped; `try_commit_bundle` already calls `_assert_tkn_round_trip`

---

## Verbatim CMD-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T9, line 3136)

**CMD-V3-01 — Command-bundle apply API**

_Source: User reframe 2026-05-08 ("the AI features are designed as API / CLI tools"); §L Locked Decisions (deterministic kernel boundary). Reframes the previous "agent edit loop" WP into a kernel API — the loop logic moves out to the agent; we ship the deterministic apply primitive._

**Status.** `now`. **Scope.** A deterministic kernel API + CLI where any caller submits a `CommandBundle{ commands, assumptions, parent_revision, targetOptionId? }` and gets back `BundleResult{ applied, violations, new_revision }`. The "loop" — propose → dry-run → read advisories → repair → commit — is what an _agent_ does using this API. We ship the API, not the loop. This is the cleanest expression of §L: bim-ai's binary contains zero AI; the agent composes calls using its own reasoning.

Three call modes: (1) `--dry-run` — runs SKB-05 + SKB-11 + SKB-19 + soundness pack against a hypothetical post-bundle state; returns advisories + checkpoint snapshot id; **does not mutate**. Idempotent. (2) `--commit` — same validation, then commits if no blocking advisories fire; returns new revision id; on failure, returns advisories and the bundle is _not_ applied. (3) `--commit --tolerate <advisory-class>` — explicit override; each tolerance recorded in audit log.

Every bundle MUST include a non-empty `assumptions` field (CMD-V3-02 contract), `parent_revision`, and target a Design Option that is not `main` (OPT-V3-01 invariant). The kernel rejects violators with structured advisories.

**Data model.**
```ts
type CommandBundle = {
  schemaVersion: 'cmd-v3.0';
  commands: KernelCommand[];
  assumptions: AssumptionEntry[];            // CMD-V3-02 — non-empty
  parentRevision: string;                    // optimistic concurrency
  targetOptionId?: string;                   // OPT-V3-01 — defaults to a fresh option
  tolerances?: Array<{ advisoryClass: string; reason: string }>;
};
type BundleResult = {
  schemaVersion: 'cmd-v3.0';
  applied: boolean;
  newRevision?: string;
  optionId?: string;
  violations: Advisory[];
  checkpointSnapshotId?: string;             // hand-off to VG-V3-01
};
```

**Engine.** New module `app/bim_ai/cmd/` with `apply_bundle(bundle, mode) → BundleResult`. Wraps kernel command application with the validation + assumption + revision contract. CLI: `bim-ai apply-bundle <bundle.json> --base <revision> [--dry-run | --commit]`. REST: `POST /api/v3/bundles`. Both registered per API-V3-01. Idempotent: same bundle replayed against the same parent yields the same new revision. **UI.** No direct UI. **Acceptance.** (a) Dry-run returns advisories + checkpoint id; kernel state unchanged. (b) `--commit` produces new revision + new option id; `main` untouched. (c) Bundle missing `assumptions` rejected with `assumption_log_required`. (d) Stale `parent_revision` rejected with `revision_conflict`. (e) Idempotent replay returns existing revision id with `applied: false`. (f) An external agent using only `bim-ai apply-bundle` + `bim-ai compare` can complete the 12-step refinement fixture (TST-V3-01). **Effort.** XL — 4 weeks. **Cross-theme references.** API-V3-01. T1 KRN-V3-04 (every commit lands as an option per OPT-V3-01). T5 (catalogKey resolution at apply time). CQ-04 (`engine.py` split must complete first).

---

## Setup

```bash
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
python -c "from bim_ai.tkn import encode, decode, diff; print('tkn ok')"
python -c "from bim_ai.api.registry import get_catalog; print(get_catalog().schemaVersion)"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `CommandBundle` + `BundleResult` Pydantic types — `app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py`

- `CommandBundle`: `schema_version: Literal["cmd-v3.0"]`, `commands: list[dict]`, `assumptions: list[AssumptionEntry]` (`min_length=1`), `parent_revision: int`, `target_option_id: str | None = None`, `tolerances: list[ToleranceEntry] | None = None`. All fields with camelCase `alias`.
- `BundleResult`: `schema_version`, `applied: bool`, `new_revision: int | None`, `option_id: str | None`, `violations: list[dict]`, `checkpoint_snapshot_id: str | None`.
- `AssumptionEntry` (CMD-V3-02 shape): `key: str`, `value: str | int | float | bool`, `confidence: float` (ge=0, le=1), `source: str`, `contestable: bool = True`, `evidence: str | None = None`.
- All models: `model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)`.

### 2. Dry-run / commit / tolerate modes — `app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`

Public signature: `def apply_bundle(doc: Document, bundle: CommandBundle, mode: Literal["dry_run", "commit"]) -> BundleResult`.

Ordered steps:
1. `_validate_assumptions(bundle.assumptions)` — return `assumption_log_required` / `assumption_log_malformed` / `assumption_log_duplicate_key` on failure.
2. Revision guard: `bundle.parent_revision != doc.revision` → `revision_conflict`.
3. `targetOptionId` guard (task 4).
4. Call `engine.try_commit_bundle(doc, bundle.commands)`.
5. Compute `checkpoint_snapshot_id` = SHA-256 of `sort_keys=True` JSON of post-bundle elements wire (use `doc` elements on dry-run failure, `new_doc` elements on success).
6. Mode branch: `dry_run` → return without persisting; `commit` + `ok=True` → return `BundleResult(applied=True, ...)`; `commit` + `ok=False` → return `applied=False`.

`apply_bundle` is **pure** — no DB I/O. Route layer handles persistence.

### 3. `parent_revision` optimistic-lock guard

Guard in step 2 above. Route layer (task 6) must additionally use a conditional DB update (`UPDATE model_records SET revision=N+1 WHERE id=X AND revision=N`); if 0 rows updated, return 409 `revision_conflict`.

### 4. `targetOptionId` routing

- Absent / `None` → proceed normally (commits to current model state; full OPT-V3-01 branching deferred to KRN-V3-04).
- `"main"` → reject with `direct_main_commit_forbidden`.
- Any other non-null value → reject with `option_routing_not_yet_implemented` + pointer to OPT-V3-01.
- Mark both branches `# TODO(OPT-V3-01)`.

### 5. `assumptions` structural validation

`_validate_assumptions` returns advisory dicts. Rules: non-empty array, each entry has `key` (non-empty), `confidence` in [0,1] (else `assumption_log_malformed` with entry index), unique keys (else `assumption_log_duplicate_key`). Kernel validates structure only — semantics live in the agent.

### 6. REST route `POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles`

Add to `app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`:

```python
@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/bundles")
async def apply_bundle_route(model_id: UUID, body: CommandBundleRequest,
    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session), hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub)) -> dict[str, Any]:
```

Flow: load row (404 if missing) → call `apply_bundle(doc, body.bundle, mode)` → on `applied=True` persist (`ModelRecord.document`, `.revision`), write `UndoStackRecord`, broadcast delta → return `result.model_dump(by_alias=True)`. HTTP 409 on `revision_conflict` or `assumption_log_required`. HTTP 200 for dry-run pass.

Keep `POST /api/models/{model_id}/commands/bundle` alive as a compatibility shim — do not remove it.

Update the `apply-bundle` `ToolDescriptor` in `app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`: add `assumptions`, `parentRevision`, `targetOptionId`, `tolerances` to `inputSchema`; update `outputSchema` to `BundleResult`; change `restEndpoint.path` to `/api/models/{model_id}/bundles`; update `cliExample` and `agentSafetyNotes`.

### 7. CLI `apply-bundle` replacing the stub

Replace the handler at line 1075 of `packages/cli/cli.mjs`. New arg shape:

```
bim-ai apply-bundle [file|-] --base <revision> [--dry-run | --commit]
                              [--tolerate <advisory-class>]...
                              [--assumptions <file>]
```

- `--base <revision>` required; maps to `parentRevision`.
- Default mode: `--dry-run` (agent safety — force explicit `--commit`).
- Legacy input (bare array or `{ commands: [] }`) auto-injects a synthetic `assumptions` entry with `confidence: 0`, `source: "cli-legacy"` and warns on stderr.
- POST to `/api/models/{model_id}/bundles`. Exit codes: 0 success, 2 `revision_conflict`, 3 `assumption_log_required`/`malformed`, 1 other error.
- Update `usage()` string.

### 8. Tests

- `app/tests/cmd/test_apply_bundle_types.py` — Pydantic round-trips; malformed `AssumptionEntry` rejection.
- `app/tests/cmd/test_apply_bundle_engine.py` — dry-run (state unchanged, checkpoint present), commit (revision+1, applied=true), missing assumptions, stale revision, `targetOptionId="main"`, tolerate mode, idempotent replay.
- `app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py` — FastAPI `TestClient`: 200 dry-run, 200 commit, 409 conflict, 409 missing assumptions, 422 malformed body.
- `packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh` — create model → dry-run → commit → verify revision incremented via `bim-ai snapshot`.

---

## Implementation rules (§A and §L)

- **No AI in any binary.** `apply_bundle` and `try_commit_bundle` are fully deterministic. No LLM, no inference.
- **API-V3-01 contract.** Update the existing `apply-bundle` stub in `registry.py` — do not register a second descriptor.
- **Assumptions-first (§L).** Kernel rejects structurally invalid bundles. Human scripts and agents satisfy the same contract.
- **Deterministic outputs.** Same bundle + same parent revision → same `BundleResult` every time.
- **No silent main mutation.** `targetOptionId: "main"` always rejected (guard is permanent, not a TODO).
- **Backwards compatibility.** Legacy `POST /api/models/{model_id}/commands/bundle` endpoint must continue to work.
- **Do not split `engine.py`.** CQ-04 is a separate WP. Import `try_commit_bundle` from `bim_ai.engine` as-is; all new logic lives in `app/bim_ai/cmd/`.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
pytest -x app/tests/cmd/ app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py -v
pytest -x app/tests/ -v                                   # no regressions
pnpm typecheck
pnpm format:check
# With server running on :8500:
BIM_AI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8500 bash packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8500/api/v3/tools/apply-bundle | python -m json.tool
```

All six acceptance criteria must be demonstrable interactively: (a) dry-run unchanged state + checkpoint id, (b) commit → applied+revision, (c) missing assumptions → `assumption_log_required`, (d) stale revision → `revision_conflict`, (e) idempotent replay → `revision_conflict`, (f) smoke test passes.

---

## Commit + push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/cmd/ app/bim_ai/routes_api.py app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
        packages/cli/cli.mjs app/tests/cmd/ app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py \
        packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(cmd): CMD-V3-01 apply-bundle API + CLI (WP-009)

New app/bim_ai/cmd/ module: CommandBundle + BundleResult Pydantic types,
AssumptionEntry structural validation, apply_bundle() pure function.
Three modes: dry_run (idempotent), commit, commit+tolerate.
parent_revision optimistic-lock, targetOptionId guards.
REST: POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles; legacy endpoint preserved.
CLI: --base, --dry-run|--commit, --tolerate, --assumptions; default dry-run.
ToolDescriptor in registry.py updated with full CMD-V3-01 schema.

Replaces WP-003 apply-bundle stub. Depends on WP-003 + WP-005.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply
```

---

## Final-report template

Paste into the PR description when merging:

```
## WP-009 CMD-V3-01 — ship report

Branch: feat/v3-cmd-v3-01-bundle-apply  |  PR: <URL>

Acceptance criteria
- [x] (a) Dry-run: advisories + checkpoint id; state unchanged
- [x] (b) --commit: applied:true, revision+1
- [x] (c) Missing assumptions → assumption_log_required
- [x] (d) Stale parent_revision → revision_conflict
- [x] (e) Idempotent replay → revision_conflict
- [x] (f) CLI smoke test: apply-bundle round-trip passes

New files: app/bim_ai/cmd/__init__.py, types.py, apply_bundle.py;
           app/tests/cmd/test_apply_bundle_types.py, test_apply_bundle_engine.py;
           app/tests/api/test_apply_bundle_route.py;
           packages/cli/tests/cli/apply-bundle-smoke.sh
Modified:  routes_api.py, api/registry.py, cli.mjs

Deferred (TODO markers):
- OPT-V3-01: fresh DesignOption routing (requires KRN-V3-04)
- TST-V3-01: full 12-step refinement harness (follow-on WP)
- Audit log for tolerances (T3 activity stream WP)

Tracker: §6 T9 CMD-V3-01 → change status to `done`
```

def try_commit_bundle(
    doc: Document,
    cmds_raw: list[dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    source_provider: SourceDocProvider | None = None,
) -> tuple[bool, Document | None, list[Command], list[Violation], str]:
    try:
        cmds: list[Command] = [coerce_command(c) for c in cmds_raw]
    except Exception as exc:
        return False, None, [], [], str(exc)
    cand = clone_document(doc)
    # KRN-06: backfill the singleton on every commit so persisted state always
    # has it (matches `try_commit`'s behaviour for the single-command path).
    ensure_internal_origin(cand)
    try:
        for cmd in cmds:
            apply_inplace(cand, cmd, source_provider=source_provider)
    except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:
        return False, None, cmds, [], str(exc)

    ensure_sun_settings(cand)
    violations = evaluate(cand.elements)

    # EDT-02 — reject bundles that break an error-severity locked constraint.
    # Runs after every command apply; the clone rollback is implicit because
    # we never return ``cand`` on failure.
    edt_violations = _evaluate_edt_constraint_violations(cand.elements)
    violations = violations + edt_violations

    blocking = [v for v in violations if v.blocking or v.severity == "error"]
    if blocking:
        return False, None, cmds, violations, "constraint_error"

    cand.revision = doc.revision + 1

    _assert_tkn_round_trip(cand)

    return True, cand, cmds, violations, "ok"


def _assert_tkn_round_trip(doc: Document) -> None:
    """Verify TKN encode→decode→encode produces the same sequence (determinism gate)."""
    from bim_ai.tkn import decode, encode

    seq_a = encode(doc.elements)
    replay_cmds = decode(seq_a, doc.elements)
    if replay_cmds:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"TKN round-trip failure: decode produced {len(replay_cmds)} unexpected commands"
        )
    seq_b = encode(doc.elements)
    if seq_a != seq_b:
        raise RuntimeError("TKN round-trip failure: encode is not deterministic")


def try_commit(
    doc: Document,
    cmd_raw: dict[str, Any],
    *,
    source_provider: SourceDocProvider | None = None,
) -> tuple[bool, Document | None, Command, list[Violation], str]:
    cmds = coerce_command(cmd_raw)
    cand = clone_document(doc)
    # KRN-06: backfill the singleton on every commit so persisted state always has it.
    ensure_internal_origin(cand)
    apply_inplace(cand, cmds, source_provider=source_provider)
    ensure_sun_settings(cand)

    violations = evaluate(cand.elements)

249	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
250	    if row is None:
251	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
252	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
253	    # KRN-06: backfill internal_origin for legacy models that pre-date this WP.
254	    # Read-only — we don't persist; the next command commit will pick it up.
255	    ensure_internal_origin(doc)
256	    ensure_sun_settings(doc)
257	    elements_wire = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc.elements.items()}
258	    if expandLinks:
259	        # FED-01: inline every linked source's elements with provenance markers
260	        # so renderers can ghost them. Default snapshot omits these to keep the
261	        # payload small.
262	        elements_wire = await _expand_host_links(session, doc, elements_wire)
263	    link_source_revisions = await _resolve_link_source_revisions(session, doc)
264	    out: dict[str, Any] = {
265	        "modelId": str(row.id),
266	        "revision": doc.revision,
267	        "elements": elements_wire,
268	        "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
269	    }
270	    if link_source_revisions:
271	        # FED-01 polish: per-source current revisions so the UI can render
272	        # drift badges on pinned links without an extra round-trip.
273	        out["linkSourceRevisions"] = link_source_revisions
274	    return out
275	
276	
277	async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
278	    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
279	) -> dict[str, int]:
280	    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
281	    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
282	    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""
283	
284	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
285	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
286	        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
287	            continue
288	        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
289	        if src_uuid_str in out:
290	            continue
291	        try:
292	            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
293	        except ValueError:
294	            continue
295	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
296	        if src_row is None:
297	            continue
298	        try:
299	            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
300	        except Exception:
301	            continue
302	        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
303	    return out
304	
305	
306	async def _expand_host_links(
307	    session: AsyncSession,
308	    host_doc: Document,
309	    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
310	) -> dict[str, Any]:
311	    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
312	    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.
313	
314	    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
315	    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
316	    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
317	    """
318	
319	    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}
320	
321	    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
322	        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
323	        if cache_key in cache:
324	            return cache[cache_key]
325	        try:
326	            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
327	        except ValueError:
328	            cache[cache_key] = None
329	            return None
330	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
331	        if src_row is None:
332	            cache[cache_key] = None
333	            return None
334	        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
335	        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
336	            cache[cache_key] = current
337	            return current
338	        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
339	        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
340	        try:
341	            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
342	        except HTTPException:
343	            cache[cache_key] = None
344	            return None
345	        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
346	        return doc_at
347	
348	    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
349	    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
350	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
351	        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
352	            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)
353	
354	    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
355	        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))
356	
357	    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)
358	
359	
360	async def _document_at_revision(
361	    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
362	) -> Document:
363	    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
364	    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
365	    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
366	    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
367	    what matters for diff.
368	    """
369	    if target_rev == current.revision:
370	        return clone_document(current)
371	    res = await session.execute(
372	        select(UndoStackRecord)
373	        .where(
374	            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
375	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
376	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
377	        )
378	        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
379	    )
380	    rolling = clone_document(current)
381	    for entry in res.scalars().all():
382	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
383	        if ok and new_doc is not None:
384	            rolling = new_doc
385	        else:
386	            raise HTTPException(
387	                status_code=409,
388	                detail=(
389	                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
390	                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
391	                ),
392	            )
393	    return rolling
394

396	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
397	async def model_diff(
398	    model_id: UUID,
399	    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
400	    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
401	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
402	) -> dict[str, Any]:
403	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
404	    if row is None:
405	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
406	    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)
407	
408	    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
409	    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)
410	
411	    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
412	        raise HTTPException(
413	            status_code=400,
414	            detail=(
415	                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
416	                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
417	            ),
418	        )
419	
420	    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
421	    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)
422	
423	    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
424	    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}
425	
426	    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
427	    return {
428	        "modelId": str(model_id),
429	        "fromRevision": from_rev,
430	        "toRevision": to_rev,
431	        **diff,
432	    }

787	@api_router.get(
788	    "/models/{model_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/table",
789	    response_model=None,
790	)
791	async def schedule_derived_table(
792	    model_id: UUID,
793	    schedule_id: str,
794	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
795	    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
796	    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,
797	    include_schedule_totals_csv: Annotated[bool, Query(alias="includeScheduleTotalsCsv")] = False,
798	) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:
799	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
800	    if row is None:
801	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
802	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
803	    try:
804	        payload = derive_schedule_table(doc, schedule_id)
805	    except ValueError as exc:
806	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
807	    if fmt.strip().lower() == "csv":
808	        export_payload = payload
809	        if columns and columns.strip():
810	            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
811	            if wanted:
812	                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
813	        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
814	            export_payload,
815	            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
816	        )
817	        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
818	        return PlainTextResponse(
819	            csv_body,
820	            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
821	            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
822	        )
823	    out = payload
824	    if columns and columns.strip():
825	        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
826	        if wanted:
827	            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
828	    return out

852	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
853	# FED-04 — IFC → shadow-model link import
854	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
855	
856	
857	class ImportIfcBody(BaseModel):
858	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-ifc``.
859	
860	    Either ``file_text`` (inline IFC STEP) or ``file_path`` (server-side path
861	    readable by the FastAPI process) must be supplied. ``slug`` names the new
862	    shadow-model row; ``link_name`` is the host-side display name for the
863	    auto-created ``link_model`` element. Both have sensible defaults so a
864	    minimal request just sends the IFC bytes.
865	    """
866	
867	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
868	    file_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="fileText")
869	    file_path: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filePath")
870	    slug: str = Field(default="ifc-import", min_length=1, max_length=128)
871	    link_name: str = Field(default="Linked IFC", alias="linkName")
872	
873	
874	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-ifc")
875	async def import_ifc_to_shadow_link(
876	    host_id: UUID,
877	    body: ImportIfcBody,
878	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
879	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
880	) -> dict[str, Any]:
881	    """FED-04: import an IFC file as a brand-new shadow bim-ai model + auto-
882	    create a ``link_model`` row in the host pointing at it.
883	
884	    Round-trip: parse IFC → ``authoritativeReplay_v0`` command bundle →
885	    apply to a fresh ``ModelRecord`` in the same project → run
886	    ``createLinkModel`` against the host. The shadow model is independent
887	    from then on (host edits never reach back into it; the host treats its
888	    elements as read-only renderable context per FED-01).
889	    """
890	
891	    from bim_ai.export_ifc import build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0
892	    from bim_ai.engine import (
893	        try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0,
894	        try_commit,
895	    )
896	
897	    # Resolve host first so we can mirror its project_id onto the shadow.
898	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
899	    if host_row is None:
900	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
901	
902	    # Read the IFC text. The endpoint accepts either inline text or a path.
903	    if body.file_text is not None:
904	        step_text = body.file_text
905	    elif body.file_path is not None:
906	        try:
907	            with open(body.file_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
908	                step_text = fh.read()
909	        except OSError as exc:
910	            raise HTTPException(
911	                status_code=400, detail=f"Cannot read IFC file: {exc}"
912	            ) from exc
913	    else:
914	        raise HTTPException(
915	            status_code=400,
916	            detail="import-ifc requires either fileText or filePath in the request body",
917	        )
918	
919	    sketch = build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0(step_text)
920	    if sketch.get("available") is not True:
921	        raise HTTPException(
922	            status_code=400,
923	            detail={
924	                "reason": "ifc_replay_unavailable",
925	                "ifcReason": sketch.get("reason"),
926	            },
927	        )
928	
929	    # 1. Create the shadow model row in the host's project.
930	    shadow_id = uuid4()
931	    shadow_doc: Document = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
932	    ensure_internal_origin(shadow_doc)
933	    ensure_sun_settings(shadow_doc)
934	
935	    # 2. Apply the replay bundle in-memory.
936	    ok, replayed_doc, applied_cmds, _viols, code = try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0(
937	        shadow_doc, sketch
938	    )
939	    if not ok or replayed_doc is None:
940	        raise HTTPException(
941	            status_code=400,
942	            detail={"reason": "ifc_replay_failed", "code": code},
943	        )
944	
945	    # 3. Persist the shadow model.
946	    shadow_row = ModelRecord(
947	        id=shadow_id,
948	        project_id=host_row.project_id,
949	        slug=body.slug,
950	        revision=replayed_doc.revision,
951	        document=document_to_wire(replayed_doc),
952	    )
953	    session.add(shadow_row)
954	    try:
955	        await session.flush()
956	    except IntegrityError:
957	        await session.rollback()
958	        raise HTTPException(
959	            status_code=409,
960	            detail="Shadow model slug already exists for this project — pass a unique 'slug'",
961	        ) from None
962	
963	    # 4. Build a createLinkModel command and apply it to the host.
964	    suggested_position = {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0, "zMm": 0.0}
965	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
966	    create_link = {
967	        "type": "createLinkModel",
968	        "name": body.link_name,
969	        "sourceModelId": str(shadow_id),
970	        "positionMm": suggested_position,
971	        "rotationDeg": 0.0,
972	        "originAlignmentMode": "origin_to_origin",
973	    }
974	    try:
975	        host_ok, new_host_doc, _cmd, host_viols, host_code = try_commit(host_doc, create_link)
976	    except Exception as exc:
977	        await session.rollback()
978	        raise HTTPException(
979	            status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkModel failed: {exc}"
980	        ) from exc
981	    if not host_ok or new_host_doc is None:
982	        await session.rollback()
983	        raise HTTPException(
984	            status_code=409,
985	            detail={
986	                "reason": host_code,
987	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in host_viols],
988	            },
989	        )
990	
991	    # The new link_model element id is the only one missing from doc_before.
992	    new_link_ids = set(new_host_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
993	    if len(new_link_ids) != 1:
994	        await session.rollback()
995	        raise HTTPException(
996	            status_code=500,
997	            detail="Internal: createLinkModel did not produce exactly one new element",
998	        )
999	    link_element_id = next(iter(new_link_ids))
1000	
1001	    # Persist the host. Keep the undo-stack record so the import is undoable.
1002	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_host_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1003	    host_row.revision = new_host_doc.revision
1004	    await session.commit()
1005	
1006	    # Broadcast the host's delta so connected clients pick up the link.
1007	    try:
1008	        await hub.broadcast_json(
1009	            host_id,
1010	            {
1011	                "type": "delta",
1012	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1013	                "revision": new_host_doc.revision,
1014	            },
1015	        )
1016	    except Exception:
1017	        # Hub failures must not roll back the import.
1018	        pass
1019	
1020	    return {
1021	        "linkedModelId": str(shadow_id),
1022	        "linkElementId": link_element_id,
1023	        "suggestedLinkPosition": suggested_position,
1024	        "appliedReplayCommandCount": len(applied_cmds),
1025	        "shadowModelSlug": body.slug,
1026	    }
1027

1030	# FED-04 — DXF underlay import
1031	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1032	
1033	
1034	class ImportDxfBody(BaseModel):
1035	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-dxf``.
1036	
1037	    Either ``file_path`` (server-side path readable by the FastAPI process)
1038	    must be supplied. ``level_id`` names the host level the underlay is
1039	    attached to. ``origin_mm`` / ``rotation_deg`` / ``scale_factor`` let the
1040	    caller place the linework; defaults centre on the project origin with
1041	    no rotation.
1042	    """
1043	
1044	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
1045	    file_path: str = Field(alias="filePath")
1046	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
1047	    name: str = Field(default="DXF Underlay")
1048	    origin_mm: dict[str, float] | None = Field(default=None, alias="originMm")
1049	    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
1050	    scale_factor: float = Field(default=1.0, alias="scaleFactor", gt=0)
1051	
1052	
1053	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-dxf")
1054	async def import_dxf(
1055	    host_id: UUID,
1056	    body: ImportDxfBody,
1057	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1058	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
1059	) -> dict[str, Any]:
1060	    """FED-04: parse a DXF file and materialise a ``link_dxf`` element.
1061	
1062	    The route reads the file at ``body.file_path``, runs the ``ezdxf``
1063	    parser, then dispatches a single ``createLinkDxf`` engine command on
1064	    the host. Returns the new ``link_dxf`` element id so the frontend can
1065	    open ManageLinksDialog with the new entry highlighted.
1066	    """
1067	
1068	    from pathlib import Path as _Path
1069	
1070	    from bim_ai.dxf_import import parse_dxf_to_linework
1071	
1072	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
1073	    if host_row is None:
1074	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
1075	
1076	    dxf_path = _Path(body.file_path)
1077	    if not dxf_path.is_file():
1078	        raise HTTPException(
1079	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF file not found at filePath: {body.file_path}"
1080	        )
1081	
1082	    try:
1083	        linework = parse_dxf_to_linework(dxf_path)
1084	    except Exception as exc:
1085	        raise HTTPException(
1086	            status_code=400, detail=f"DXF parse failed: {exc}"
1087	        ) from exc
1088	
1089	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
1090	    if body.level_id not in host_doc.elements or not isinstance(
1091	        host_doc.elements[body.level_id], LevelElem
1092	    ):
1093	        raise HTTPException(
1094	            status_code=400, detail="levelId must reference an existing Level on the host model"
1095	        )
1096	
1097	    create_cmd = {
1098	        "type": "createLinkDxf",
1099	        "name": body.name,
1100	        "levelId": body.level_id,
1101	        "originMm": body.origin_mm or {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0},
1102	        "rotationDeg": float(body.rotation_deg),
1103	        "scaleFactor": float(body.scale_factor),
1104	        "linework": linework,
1105	    }
1106	    try:
1107	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, viols, code = try_commit_bundle(host_doc, [create_cmd])
1108	    except Exception as exc:
1109	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkDxf failed: {exc}") from exc
1110	    if not ok or new_doc is None:
1111	        raise HTTPException(
1112	            status_code=409,
1113	            detail={
1114	                "reason": code,
1115	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in viols],
1116	            },
1117	        )
1118	
1119	    new_link_dxf_ids = [
1120	        eid
1121	        for eid in set(new_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1122	        if getattr(new_doc.elements[eid], "kind", None) == "link_dxf"
1123	    ]
1124	    if len(new_link_dxf_ids) != 1:
1125	        raise HTTPException(
1126	            status_code=500,
1127	            detail="Internal: createLinkDxf did not produce exactly one new link_dxf element",
1128	        )
1129	    link_element_id = new_link_dxf_ids[0]
1130	
1131	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1132	    host_row.revision = new_doc.revision
1133	    await session.commit()
1134	
1135	    try:
1136	        await hub.broadcast_json(
1137	            host_id,
1138	            {
1139	                "type": "delta",
1140	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1141	                "revision": new_doc.revision,
1142	            },
1143	        )
1144	    except Exception:
1145	        pass
1146	
1147	    return {
1148	        "linkedElementId": link_element_id,
1149	        "lineworkCount": len(linework),
1150	    }
1151

1177	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1178	# WebSocket
1179	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1180	
1181	
1182	async def websocket_loop(websocket: WebSocket, model_id: UUID, hub: Hub) -> None:
1183	
1184	    sid = str(model_id)
1185	
1186	    async with SessionMaker() as session:
1187	        row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
1188	
1189	    await websocket.accept()
1190	
1191	    if row is None:
1192	        await websocket.close(code=4404)
1193	
1194	        return
1195	
1196	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
1197	
1198	    hub.subscribe(sid, websocket)
1199	
1200	    await websocket.send_json(
1201	        {
1202	            "type": "snapshot",
1203	            "modelId": sid,
1204	            "revision": doc.revision,
1205	            "elements": {k: el.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, el in doc.elements.items()},
1206	            "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
1207	        },
1208	    )
1209	
1210	    try:
1211	        while True:
1212	            msg = await websocket.receive_json()
1213	
1214	            mt = msg.get("type")
1215	
1216	            if mt == "presence_update":
1217	                pid = msg.get("peerId")
1218	
1219	                if not isinstance(pid, str) or not pid:
1220	                    continue
1221	
1222	                hub.set_peer_id(websocket, pid)
1223	
1224	                patch = {str(k): v for k, v in msg.items() if k != "type"}
1225	
1226	                hub.touch_presence(sid, pid, patch)
1227	
1228	                await hub.broadcast_presence(sid)
1229	
1230	            elif mt == "presence":
1231	                # legacy noop relay
1232	
1233	                payload = msg.get("payload", {})
1234	
1235	                await hub.broadcast_json(
1236	                    sid, {"type": "presence", "modelId": sid, "payload": payload}
1237	                )
1238	
1239	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
1240	        pass
1241	
1242	    finally:
1243	        hub.unregister(websocket)
1244

1332	class TknDiffRequest(BaseModel):

1338	@api_router.post("/models/{model_id}/tokens/diff")
1339	async def tokens_diff(
1340	    model_id: UUID,
1341	    body: TknDiffRequest,
1342	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
1343	) -> dict[str, Any]:

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Annotated, Any
from uuid import UUID, uuid4

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, TypeAdapter
from sqlalchemy import desc, select
from sqlalchemy.exc import IntegrityError
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from bim_ai.agent_brief_acceptance_readout import agent_brief_acceptance_readout_v1
from bim_ai.agent_brief_command_protocol import agent_brief_command_protocol_v1
from bim_ai.agent_evidence_review_loop import agent_review_actions_v1, bcf_topics_index_v1
from bim_ai.agent_generated_bundle_qa_checklist import (
    agent_generated_bundle_qa_checklist_v1,
)
from bim_ai.agent_review_readout_consistency_closure import (
    agent_review_readout_consistency_closure_v1,
)
from bim_ai.codes import BUILDING_PRESETS
from bim_ai.commands import Command
from bim_ai.db import SessionMaker, get_session
from bim_ai.diff_engine import compute_element_diff
from bim_ai.document import Document
from bim_ai.elements import Element, LevelElem, LinkModelElem, PlanViewElem
from bim_ai.engine import clone_document, ensure_internal_origin, ensure_sun_settings, try_commit_bundle
from bim_ai.agent_loop import (
    AGENT_BACKEND_ENV_VAR,
    AgentIterateRequest,
    AgentIterateResponse,
    generate_patch,
)
from bim_ai.family_catalog_format import (
    load_catalog_by_id,
    load_catalog_index,
)
from bim_ai.evidence_manifest import (
    MINIMAL_PROBE_PNG_BYTES_V1,
    MINIMAL_PROBE_PNG_CANONICAL_SHA256_V1,
    agent_evidence_closure_hints,
    artifact_upload_manifest_v1,
    deterministic_3d_view_evidence_manifest,
    deterministic_plan_view_evidence_manifest,
    deterministic_section_cut_evidence_manifest,
    deterministic_sheet_evidence_manifest,
    evidence_agent_follow_through_v1,
    evidence_baseline_lifecycle_readout_v1,
    evidence_closure_review_v1,
    evidence_diff_ingest_fix_loop_v1,
    evidence_lifecycle_signal_v1,
    evidence_package_digest_invariants_v1,
    evidence_package_semantic_digest_sha256,
    evidence_review_performance_gate_v1,

)

from bim_ai.routes_activity import activity_router

    PERSPECTIVE_IDS,

)
from bim_ai.routes_exports import exports_router
from bim_ai.routes_sketch import sketch_router
from bim_ai.schedule_csv import schedule_payload_to_csv, schedule_payload_with_column_subset
from bim_ai.schedule_derivation import derive_schedule_table, list_schedule_ids
from bim_ai.sheet_preview_svg import SHEET_PRINT_RASTER_PRINT_SURROGATE_CONTRACT_V2
from bim_ai.tables import ModelRecord, ProjectRecord, UndoStackRecord
from bim_ai.template_loader import (
    list_templates,

    template_exists,
)
